summary: mermaid-v11 preinstall exfiltrates installer host/user to attacker OOB collector
details: The package impersonates the legitimate 'mermaid' diagramming library and publishes at version 9999.0.1 to win dependency-confusion resolution. It ships no library code — only a manifest with a malicious preinstall hook. On `npm install`, package.json line 6 runs `node -e` to read `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username` and sends them via HTTPS GET to `https://d8ks495t5p5ut2enft80bhuqt1s13pm5j.oast.site/?h=<host>&u=<user>&pkg=mermaid-v11`, plus a DNS lookup encoding the hostname as a subdomain of oast.site. The destination is an interactsh-style out-of-band collector controlled by the attacker. This auto-fires on default install with no user action required, leaks installer identity to attacker infrastructure, and primes the host for follow-on targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
